sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: ὦ τέκνα Δαναῶν , πρὶν κατεξάνθαι βολαῖς ,
sections.translation: You sons of Danaus , before you are torn to pieces by their attack , why delay to fall upon the gates with all your might , light-armed and cavalry and charioteers ? No loitering then , soon as they heard that call ; and many fell with bloody head ,

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: ἡμῶν τʼ ἐς οὖδας εἶδες ἂν πρὸ τειχέων
sections.translation: and many of us you could have seen thrown to the earth like tumblers before the walls , breathing their last , bedewing the dry ground with streams of blood .

sections.label: Section 1153
sections.original: ὁ δʼ Ἀρκάς , οὐκ Ἀργεῖος , Ἀταλάντης γόνος
sections.translation: Then Atalanta ’ s son , who was not an Argive but an Arcadian , hurling himself like a hurricane at the gates , called for

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original: πῦρ καὶ δικέλλας , ὡς κατασκάψων πόλιν ·
sections.translation: fire and picks to raze the town ; but Periclymenus , son of the ocean-god , stayed his wild career , heaving on his head a wagon-load of stone , the coping from the battlements ; and it shattered his head with yellow hair and

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: ἔρρηξεν ὀστέων , ἄρτι δʼ οἰνωπὸν γένυν
sections.translation: crashed through the seams of the skull , dabbling with blood his fresh cheek ; and he will never go back alive to his mother with her lovely bow , the maid of Maenalus .

sections.label: Section 1163
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δὲ τάσδʼ ἐσεῖδεν εὐτυχεῖς πύλας ,
sections.translation: Your son then , seeing these gates secure , went on to the next , and I followed him .

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: ὁρῶ δὲ Τυδέα καὶ παρασπιστὰς πυκνοὺς
sections.translation: I saw Tydeus and his thick rows of targeteers hurling their Aetolian spears into the opening at the top of the turrets , so that our men fled and left the battlements ; but your son rallied them once more , as a huntsman cheers his hounds ,

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: πύργοις δʼ ἐπέστησʼ αὖθις . ἐς δʼ ἄλλας πύλας
sections.translation: and stationed them at the towers again . And then we hastened to other gates , after stopping the affliction there . As for the madness of Capaneus , how can I describe it ? He was going about with a long scaling-ladder , and boasting

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: μηδʼ ἂν τὸ σεμνὸν πῦρ νιν εἰργαθεῖν Διὸς
sections.translation: that even the holy fire of Zeus would not hold him back from giving the city to utter destruction . And even as he spoke , he climbed up beneath the hail of stones , crouched under the shelter of his shield , rung by smooth rung going up the ladder .

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: ἤδη δʼ ὑπερβαίνοντα γεῖσα τειχέων
sections.translation: But , just as he was scaling the parapet of the wall , Zeus smote him with a thunderbolt ; the earth re-echoed , and fear seized everyone ; for from the ladder his limbs were slung far apart , his head toward Olympus , his blood toward earth ,

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: χεῖρες δὲ καὶ κῶλʼ ὡς κύκλωμʼ Ἰξίονος
sections.translation: while his legs and arms went spinning round like Ixion ’ s wheel he was hurled , spinnning ; his burning corpse fell to the ground .

sections.label: Section 1187
sections.original: ὡς δʼ εἶδʼ Ἄδραστος Ζῆνα πολέμιον στρατῷ ,
sections.translation: But when Adrastus saw that Zeus was hostile to his army , he drew the Argive troops outside the trench . Meanwhile our armed cavalry , seeing the lucky omen of Zeus before us ,

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: ἰδόντες ἐξήλαυνον ἁρμάτων ὄχους
sections.translation: were driving forth their chariots , and the armed men charged with spears into the middle of the Argives , and all troubles happened at once : men were dying , hurled headlong from chariots , wheels flew off , axles crashed together ,

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: νεκροὶ δὲ νεκροῖς ἐξεσωρεύονθʼ ὁμοῦ .
sections.translation: while the dead were heaped up on the dead . So for to-day we have prevented destruction of the towers of our land ; but if this land will be fortunate for the future , that rests with the gods ; for even now it owes its safety to some deity .

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: καλὸν τὸ νικᾶν · εἰ δʼ ἀμείνονʼ οἱ θεοὶ
sections.translation: Victory is fair ; and if the gods are growing kinder , it would be well with me .

sections.label: Section 1202
sections.original: καλῶς τὰ τῶν θεῶν καὶ τὰ τῆς τύχης ἔχει ·
sections.translation: The gods and fortune have treated us well ; for my sons are alive and my land has escaped ruin . But Creon seems to have had bitter enjoyment

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: τῶν τʼ Οἰδίπου δύστηνος ἀπολαῦσαι κακῶν ,
sections.translation: from my marriage with Oedipus , by losing his son to his sorrow , a public success , a private grief . But please , come back to your tale again and say what my two sons intend to do next .

sections.label: Section 1209
sections.original: ἔα τὰ λοιπά · δεῦρʼ ἀεὶ γὰρ εὐτυχεῖς .
sections.translation: Let the rest be ; all is well with you so far .

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: τοῦτʼ εἰς ὕποπτον εἶπας · οὐκ ἐατέον .
sections.translation: Your words rouse my suspicions ; I cannot let it be .

sections.label: Section 1211
sections.original: μεῖζον τί χρῄζεις παῖδας ἢ σεσῳσμένους ;
sections.translation: Have you any further wish than your sons ’ safety ?

sections.label: Section 1212
sections.original: καὶ τἀπίλοιπά γʼ εἰ καλῶς πράσσω κλύειν .
sections.translation: Yes , to hear if I shall fare well in the future .

sections.label: Section 1213
sections.original: μέθες μʼ · ἔρημος παῖς ὑπασπιστοῦ σέθεν .
sections.translation: Let me go ; your son is left without his squire .

sections.label: Section 1214
sections.original: κακόν τι κεύθεις καὶ στέγεις ὑπὸ σκότῳ .
sections.translation: There is some evil you are hiding , veiling it in darkness .

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: κοὐκ ἄν γε λέξαιμʼ ἐπʼ ἀγαθοῖσι σοῖς κακά .
sections.translation: I would not add ill news to the good you have heard .

sections.label: Section 1216
sections.original: ἢν μή γε φεύγων ἐκφύγῃς πρὸς αἰθέρα .
sections.translation: You must , unless you take wings and fly away .

sections.label: Section 1217
sections.original: αἰαῖ · τί μʼ οὐκ εἴασας ἐξ εὐαγγέλου
sections.translation: Ah ! Why did you not let me go after my good news , instead of forcing me to disclose evil ? Those two sons of yours are resolved on

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original: αἴσχιστα , χωρὶς μονομαχεῖν παντὸς στρατοῦ ,
sections.translation: deeds of shameful recklessness , a single combat apart from the army ; they addressed to Argives and Thebans alike words I would they had never uttered . Eteocles , taking his stand on a lofty tower , after ordering silence to be proclaimed to the army , began :

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: ἔλεξε δʼ · ὦ γῆς Ἑλλάδος στρατηλάται
sections.translation: and said : O captains of Hellas , chieftains of Argos here assembled , and you people of Cadmus , do not barter your lives for Polyneices or for me ! For I myself excuse you from this risk ,

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: μόνος συνάψω συγγόνῳ τὠμῷ μάχην ·
sections.translation: and will engage my brother in single combat ; and if I slay him , I will possess my house alone , but if I am conquered I will hand down the city to him alone . You men of Argos , give up the struggle and return to your land , do not lose your lives here ;

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: Σπαρτῶν τε λαὸς ἅλις ὅσος κεῖται νεκρός .
sections.translation: there are enough of the Sown-men who lie dead .

sections.label: Section 1236
sections.original: τοσαῦτʼ ἔλεξε · σὸς δὲ Πολυνείκης γόνος
sections.translation: So he spoke ; then your son Polyneices rushed from the battle-line and assented to his proposal . And all the Argives and the people of Cadmus shouted their approval , as though they thought it just .

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: ἐπὶ τοῖσδε δʼ ἐσπείσαντο , κἀν μεταιχμίοις
sections.translation: On these terms the armies made a truce , and in the space between them the generals took an oath to abide by .

sections.label: Section 1242
sections.original: ἤδη δʼ ἔκρυπτον σῶμα παγχάλκοις ὅπλοις
sections.translation: At once , the two sons of the old Oedipus were hiding themselves in bronze armor ; and lords of Thebes with friendly care equipped the captain of this land ,

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: Σπαρτῶν ἀριστῆς , τὸν δὲ Δαναϊδῶν ἄκροι .
sections.translation: while Argive chieftains armed the other . There they stood dazzling , nor were they pale , all eagerness to hurl their lances at each other . Then their friends came to their sides first one , then another , with words of encouragement , saying :

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: Πολύνεικες , ἐν σοὶ Ζηνὸς ὀρθῶσαι βρέτας
sections.translation: Polyneices , it rests with you to set up an image of Zeus as a trophy and crown Argos with fair renown .

sections.label: Section 1252
sections.original: Ἐτεοκλέα δʼ αὖ · νῦν πόλεως ὑπερμαχεῖς ,
sections.translation: Others to Eteocles : Now you are fighting for your city ; now , if victorious , you have the scepter in your power .

sections.label: Section 1254
sections.original: τάδʼ ἠγόρευον παρακαλοῦντες ἐς μάχην .
sections.translation: So they spoke , cheering them to the battle .

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.original: μάντεις δὲ μῆλʼ ἔσφαζον , ἐμπύρους τʼ ἀκμὰς
sections.translation: The seers were sacrificing sheep and noting the tongues and forks of fire , the damp reek which is a bad omen , and the tapering flame which gives decisions on two points , being both a sign of victory and defeat .

sections.label: Section 1259
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , εἴ τινʼ ἀλκὴν ἢ σοφοὺς ἔχεις λόγους
sections.translation: But , if you have any power or subtle speech

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: ἢ φίλτρʼ ἐπῳδῶν , στεῖχʼ , ἐρήτυσον τέκνα
sections.translation: or charmed spell , go , restrain your children from this terrible combat , for great is the risk they run . The prize of the contest will be grievous sorrow for you , if to-day you are deprived of both your sons . Exit Messenger .

sections.label: Section 1264
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον ἔξελθʼ Ἀντιγόνη δόμων πάρος ·
sections.translation: Antigone , my daughter , come out of the house ;

sections.label: Section 1265
sections.original: οὐκ ἐν χορείαις οὐδὲ παρθενεύμασι
sections.translation: this heaven-sent crisis is no time for dances or girlish pursuits . But you and your mother must prevent two brave men , your own brothers , from plunging into death and falling by each other ’ s hand .

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.original: τίνʼ , ὦ τεκοῦσα μῆτερ , ἔκπληξιν νέαν
sections.translation: Mother , what new terror are you proclaiming to your friends before the palace ?

sections.label: Section 1272
sections.original: ὦ θύγατερ , ἔρρει σῶν κασιγνήτων βίος .
sections.translation: Daughter , your brothers ’ lives are going to ruin .

sections.label: Section 1273
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας ;
sections.translation: What do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 1273b
sections.original: αἰχμὴν ἐς μίαν καθέστατον .
sections.translation: They have resolved on single combat .

sections.label: Section 1274
sections.original: οἲ ʼγώ , τί λέξεις , μῆτερ ;
sections.translation: Oh no ! what do you have to say , mother ?

sections.label: Section 1274b
sections.original: οὐ φίλʼ , ἀλλʼ ἕπου .
sections.translation: No welcome news ; follow me .

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.original: ποῖ , παρθενῶνας ἐκλιποῦσʼ ;
sections.translation: Where , away from my maiden ’ s chamber ?

sections.label: Section 1275b
sections.original: ἀνὰ στρατόν .
sections.translation: To the army .

sections.label: Section 1276
sections.original: αἰδούμεθʼ ὄχλον .
sections.translation: I cannot face the crowd .

sections.label: Section 1276b
sections.original: οὐκ ἐν αἰσχύνῃ τὰ σά .
sections.translation: Coyness is not for you now .

sections.label: Section 1277
sections.original: δράσω δὲ δὴ τί ;
sections.translation: But what shall I do ?

sections.label: Section 1277b
sections.original: συγγόνων λύσεις ἔριν .
sections.translation: You will put an end to your brothers ’ strife .

sections.label: Section 1278
sections.original: τί δρῶσα , μῆτερ ;
sections.translation: How so , mother ?

sections.label: Section 1278b
sections.original: προσπίτνουσʼ ἐμοῦ μέτα .
sections.translation: By falling at their knees with me .

sections.label: Section 1279
sections.original: ἡγοῦ σὺ πρὸς μεταίχμιʼ · οὐ μελλητέον .
sections.translation: Lead on till we are between the armies ; we must not delay .

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: ἔπειγʼ ἔπειγε , θύγατερ · ὡς , ἢν μὲν φθάσω
sections.translation: Haste , my daughter , haste ! For , if I can forestall the onset of my sons , I may yet live ; but if they are dead , I will lie down in death with them . Exeunt Jocasta and Antigone .

sections.label: Section 1.1284
sections.original: αἰαῖ αἰαῖ , τρομερὰν φρίκᾳ
sections.translation: Alas , alas ! My mind is trembling with fear ,

sections.label: Section 1.1285
sections.original: τρομερὰν φρένʼ ἔχω · διὰ σάρκα δʼ ἐμὰν
sections.translation: trembling ; and through my flesh goes a throb of pity , of pity for the hapless mother . Which of her two sons will stain the other with blood —
